summary: Implement M4 campaign grain supply reservations and consumption forecast
acceptance_criteria: Campaign plans can reserve, release, and consume grain supply from existing M2 resource state through explicit commands/events without creating resources from nothing.
Supply records distinguish stockpile source, reserved amount, carried amount, consumed amount, shortage, spoilage or loss reason if present, and expected days of supply.
Forecast queries show grain needs for planned muster and march timing, including reason codes for insufficient grain and seasonal risk.
Supply consumption is deterministic across equal inputs and uses stable tie-breakers when multiple sources can satisfy a demand.
The task does not implement route capacity, naval logistics, full market economy, UI behavior, or battle/siege outcomes.
required_tests: Property/invariant tests cover grain conservation, no negative supply, deterministic source allocation, reservation release, and shortage reason ordering.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-GRAIN-SUPPLY-001
notes: This task should reuse M2 grain/economy scaffolds and keep the M4 model explicit and serializable.
Do not introduce runtime SQL, object graph serialization, or a generic resource manager.

Explicit exclusions for M4 remain: manual node battle, complex naval warfare, full diplomacy, 3D battle, multiplayer/server, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, and frozen product or architecture changes.
